raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, my card from Priceline Visa Barclays was with a fraud charge from XXXX notary in Pennsylvania for {$110.00}. I called the credit card card company and the merchant XXXX. 
XXXX XXXX responded back and tried to refund the credit card. The company issued a refund then they charged back a {$110.00} again. I asked the credit company to do a stop payment so there wont be repeated charges from XXXX. The credit card company has not complied. The credit card company is making a fraud issue when everything has been resolved with merchant. Visa Priceline Barclays should be applying the credit offered by the merchant but has been hanging up the phone calls and offering agents with deep accents or foreign accents. Can someone help make the credit card company comply with the rules? Thank you. They should not leave me in a balance of {$110.00} when the merchant is calling them with refund information. I should be at XXXX balance on this credit card,
